# Baseline RF vs published references (TSF, 1NN-DTW)

Per row the five accuracy cells are ranked **within the row** (green = 1st, light-green = 2nd, yellow = 3rd, orange = 4th, red = 5th). All other numeric cells carry an **across-dataset rank in parentheses**: `(1)` is the dataset on which the value is **most demanding for our pipeline**. By default this means the *highest* value (more features, more leaves, larger EU). **Exception:** for the two σ columns (EU&nbsp;σ and leaves&nbsp;σ) `(1)` is the *lowest* σ — uniform medium-rich features compound multiplicatively and are harder for our pipeline, so low σ is the demanding regime.
